company.name: RAIN
company.headline: Rainwater infrastructure, delivered as a managed service.
company.tagline: Harvest, process, certify, and distribute non-potable water with one connected system.
company.mission: RAIN turns rooftops and surface runoff into monitored, certified water for construction, agriculture, industry, and municipalities.

stats.label: Integrated layers
stats.detail: Collection, processing, and the RaaS platform.

stats.label: Live monitoring
stats.detail: Sensor telemetry, alerts, and water-quality visibility.

layers.title: Collection Layer
layers.summary: Capture rain where it falls and route it into a measurable supply chain.
features: Roof catchment and modular collector hardware
Surface flow routing for industrial and urban sites
Reservoir feed planning based on rainfall events

layers.title: Processing Layer
layers.summary: Treat harvested water to a reliable non-potable quality standard.
features: UV sterilization for stable output quality
Quality sensors for turbidity and operational checks

layers.title: RaaS Platform
layers.summary: Operate the infrastructure as Water-as-a-Service with digital control and billing.
features: Order, invoicing, and service workflow automation
Usage tracking for recurring and volume-based pricing

capabilities.title: Quality intelligence
capabilities.copy: Track collection conditions, processing health, and output quality from one command layer.

capabilities.title: Operational automation
capabilities.copy: Coordinate collectors, reservoirs, treatment steps, and delivery events without manual spreadsheets.

capabilities.title: Commercial engine
capabilities.copy: Convert treated rainwater into a billable service with flexible contracts and usage data.

sectors.name: Construction
sectors.benefit: Lower freshwater demand on temporary or high-consumption job sites.

sectors.name: Agriculture
sectors.benefit: Create a more resilient water source for planned irrigation cycles.

sectors.name: Industry
sectors.benefit: Reduce utility dependency for repetitive non-potable water demand.

sectors.name: Municipalities
sectors.benefit: Support greener city services with visible sustainability metrics.

revenue_model.name: Subscription plans
revenue_model.detail: Recurring infrastructure and monitoring contracts for predictable monthly revenue.

revenue_model.name: Volume pricing
revenue_model.detail: Usage-based charges for delivered or processed water by consumption tier.

revenue_model.name: Premium quality tariffs
revenue_model.detail: Higher-margin service levels for sectors that need tighter monitoring and reporting.

roadmap.title: Capture
roadmap.copy: Deploy modular collection hardware on rooftops, surfaces, and storage points.

roadmap.title: Treat
roadmap.copy: Filter, sterilize, and verify water before it reaches the usable inventory pool.

roadmap.title: Monetize
roadmap.copy: Sell verified non-potable water through contracts, dashboards, and automated billing.

faq.question: What does RAIN actually sell?
faq.answer: RAIN sells managed non-potable water capacity: the infrastructure, monitoring, and service layer around harvested rainwater.

faq.question: Why focus on non-potable applications first?
faq.answer: Construction, irrigation, cooling, washing, and municipal maintenance can unlock strong demand without the complexity of drinking-water certification.

faq.question: How is quality tracked?
faq.answer: The platform combines treatment checkpoints, sensor inputs, and a real-time dashboard so customers can see what is happening inside the system.
